topic.name: U.S. Government Appropriations
topic.description: The documents discuss various appropriations and authorizations for U.S. government departments and programs, including foreign relations, defense, and social services.

Document 1: Authorizes FY2010-FY2011 appropriations for the Department of State to carry diplomatic and consular affairs
Authorizes FY2010-FY2011 appropriations for contributions to international organizations
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2008 for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force
Authorizes appropriations for defense-wide procurement and National Guard and reserve equipment
Document 3: Revises the Older Americans Act of 1965 to include LGBT individuals within the status of greatest social need
Expands the duties of the Assistant Secretary to include assisting the states with the development of Home Care Consumer Bills of Rights

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Tariffs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes related to social security and tariff adjustments in the United States.

Document 1: Amnesty Bonuses Elimination Act
prohibit the Social Security Administration from issuing a Social Security account number to any alien granted deferred action
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
duty treatment of yttrium oxides having a purity of at least 99.9 percent
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
increase the duty on N-[2,4-dichloro-5-[4-(difluoromethyl)-4,5-dihydro-3-methyl-5-oxo-1H-1,2,4-triazol-1-yl]phenyl]methanesulfonamide

topic.name: Election and Broadcasting Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating election processes and broadcasting standards in the United States.

Document 1: prohibit the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) from having the authority to require broadcasters to present opposing viewpoints
commonly referred to as the Fairness Doctrine.
Document 2: repeal the limitation on party expenditures on behalf of candidates in general elections
Document 3: establish the first Saturday and Sunday after the first Friday in November as the days for the election
polling place hours in the United States for both congressional and presidential elections

topic.name: Tariff Suspension Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments related to the temporary suspension of tariffs on specific chemical compounds and materials in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on 3-oxido-5-oxo-4-propionylcyclohex-3-enecarboxylic acid calcium salt.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to temporarily suspend the duty on 4-vinylbenzenesulfonic acid, lithium salt.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on a certain ion exchange resin.

topic.name: Government Data and Privacy Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing the accessibility of government data while also addressing privacy and security concerns related to personal information.

Document 1: requires government data assets made available by federal agencies to be published as machine-readable data
Agencies must make their enterprise data inventories available to the public on Data.gov
Document 2: amends the Communications Act of 1934 to expand the prohibition against knowingly transmitting inaccurate caller identification information
The FCC must prescribe regulations that require providers of telecommunications services to enable technology for subscribers to verify the accuracy of their caller identification information
Document 3: provides criminal penalties for intentional failures to provide required notices of a security breach involving sensitive personally identifiable information
Requires federal agencies as part of their rulemaking process to prepare and make available to the public privacy impact assessments

topic.name: Healthcare Legislation and Policy
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative amendments related to healthcare services, including occupational therapy, Medicaid, and Medicare provisions.

Document 1: Occupational therapists within the definition of 'behavioral and mental health professionals'
National Health Service Corps.
Document 2: Amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2011
increase in the federal medical assistance percentage (FMAP) under SSA title XIX.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
ownership of a Group 3 Support Surface item of complex durable medical equipment (DME) after the 13-month capped rental period ends.

topic.name: Medicare Payment and Coverage Reforms
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at reforming Medicare payment structures, enhancing billing transparency, and regulating health insurance coverage.

Document 1: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
make payment adjustments for subsection (d) hospitals
Document 2: repeals Title I of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
amends the Public Health Service Act to provide that the laws of the state designated by a health insurance issuer shall apply
Document 3: requires a critical access hospital or other hospital to comply with certain requirements related to billing for out-of-network services
must provide notice as to whether the hospital is not within the health care provider network

topic.name: Regulatory Acts and Health Safety
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative measures aimed at regulating health safety standards and practices in various domains.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to establish a process for the review and approval of over-the-counter (OTC) sunscreen active ingredients.
Requires the Secretary to review any request regarding a sunscreen active ingredient or combination of ingredients that has been in use.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Defense to establish procedures for the predeployment notification to members of the Armed Forces of any known or likely use of depleted uranium.
Requires the Secretary of each military department to establish procedures for collecting, tracking, and maintaining information on the health status of individuals tested under this Act.
Document 3: Amends the Departments of Commerce, Justice, and State, the Judiciary, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2001 to require the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to modify the rules authorizing the operation of low-power FM radio stations.
States that any license that was issued by the FCC to a low-power FM station prior to April 2, 2001, and that does not comply with the modifications adopted by the Commission shall remain invalid.

topic.name: Environmental Regulation and Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures and assessments aimed at managing environmental quality and impacts through regulatory frameworks.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to authorize grants for water pollution control projects
Technical assistance to rural and small municipalities for water quality protection.
Document 2: Establishes the Cumulative Regulatory Assessment Committee to assess environmental regulations
Assesses economic impacts of federal regulatory mandates on various sectors.
Document 3: Requires the EPA to provide grants for research on managing environmental impacts of electronic devices
Focuses on recycling, reuse, and reduction of hazardous materials in electronic devices.

topic.name: Immigration and Criminal Justice Reform
topic.description: Legislation addressing the intersection of immigration laws and criminal justice, particularly focusing on the detention and removal of aliens and the notification requirements for sex offenders traveling internationally.

Document 1: requires sex offenders required to register under such Act to notify their residential jurisdictions of their intent to travel from the United States to another country
establishes a Cyber Crimes Center (CCC) to address criminal acts crossing U.S. borders through cyberspace
Document 2: establish criminal penalties for an alien who is permitted to depart voluntarily and subsequently enters, attempts to enter, or is present in the United States unlawfully
revises criminal penalties for the reentry of certain aliens who were removed from the United States for specified criminal-related grounds
Document 3: expands the authority of the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to take a criminal alien into custody pending a determination of removability
limits the Attorney General's review of DHS custody determinations to whether the alien may be detained, released with no bond, or released on bond of at least $1,500

topic.name: Military and Homeland Security Funding
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the appropriations and funding for military construction, homeland security initiatives, and maritime administration efforts.

Document 1: Appropriates funds for FY2011 for the Department of Defense (DOD) for military construction for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force.
Prohibits appropriated funds from being used to initiate a new installation overseas without prior notification to the congressional appropriations committees.
Document 2: This bill directs the Comptroller General to conduct an assessment of Department of Homeland Security (DHS) personnel assigned to fusion centers.
Fusion centers serve as a focal point for the receipt, analysis, gathering, and sharing of threat-related information.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Human Rights
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of U.S. foreign policy, including trade relations, child welfare, and human rights sanctions.

Document 1: Prohibits the President from restricting direct transfers from a Cuban depository institution to a U.S. depository institution in payment for a product authorized for sale under the Trade Sanctions Reform and Export Enhancement Act of 2000.
Expresses the sense of Congress that temporary entry visas should be issued to Cuban nationals to facilitate purchase of U.S. agricultural products.
Document 2: The President shall appoint an Ambassador-at-Large to promote oversight of the VCFS;
advise the State Department and the President about vulnerable children and family security in foreign countries.
Document 3: Directs the President to impose financial and immigration/entry sanctions on listed nationals of Vietnam who are complicit in human rights abuses.
Terminates sanctions if the President certifies to Congress that the government of Vietnam has released all political prisoners.

topic.name: Tax Reform Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at simplifying and restructuring various aspects of the tax system in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the wages or other remuneration earned by an employee who performs employment duties in more than one state from being subject to income tax in any state other than the state of the employee's residence.
Exempts employers from withholding of tax and information reporting requirements for employees not subject to income tax under this Act.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to replace the marginal income tax rates with a single rate of 17% on individual taxable income.
Replaces the current tax on corporations with a tax on every person engaged in a business activity equal to 17% of the business taxable income of such person.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the $1 million limitation on the deductibility of employee compensation.
Eliminate the exemption from such limitation for compensation payable on a commission basis or upon the attainment of a performance goal.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments and acts related to environmental policy, international relations, and health services in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973
shall not apply to the gray wolf (canis lupus)
Document 2: Prohibiting Discrimination Against Israel Act
no funds authorized or appropriated by federal law may be expended for any international project that prohibits participation of Israeli organizations
Document 3: National Health Service Corps Scholarship and Loan Repayment Programs Reauthorization Act of 2007
reauthorize appropriations for FY2007-FY2011 for National Health Service Corps Scholarship and Loan Repayment programs

topic.name: Workforce Development in Energy Sector
topic.description: The documents focus on initiatives and policies aimed at enhancing education, training, and workforce development specifically in the energy and manufacturing sectors.

Document 1: This bill directs the Department of Energy (DOE), in awarding grants, to prioritize education and training for energy and manufacturing jobs
DOE must prioritize educating and training workers from underrepresented groups such as minorities, women, and veterans.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at promoting and advancing renewable energy technologies and practices.

Document 1: establish a research and development program to provide lower cost and more viable thermal energy storage technologies
a program of grants to states to demonstrate advanced photovoltaic technology.
Document 2: establish a local farm business and market garden loan program to assist producers in establishing local farm business projects
create local employment opportunities.
Document 3: allow an energy tax credit for investment in waste heat to power property
generates electricity through the recovery of a qualified waste heat resource.

topic.name: Financial Regulation Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments related to the regulation and oversight of financial institutions, particularly concerning capital requirements and depositor protections.

Document 1: amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act
require the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC), the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC), and the Federal Reserve Board
Document 2: Prohibits the Secretary of the Treasury from requiring a payor of interest to file an information return
paid to a nonresident alien on a deposit maintained at an office within the United States
Document 3: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to limit the authority of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC)
Requires an Act of Congress to declare that severe financial conditions exist

topic.name: Public Land Management and Use
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, disposal, and acquisition of public lands in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Land Transaction Facilitation Act to reauthorize the program for the completion of appraisals and satisfaction of other legal requirements for the sale or exchange of public land.
Directs the Department of the Interior to establish and maintain a database containing a comprehensive list of all of the public land identified for disposal.
Document 2: Directs the Department of Veterans Affairs to complete environmental, cultural resource, and other due diligence activities on certain public land.
The land shall be withdrawn from all forms of appropriation under the public land laws for as long as it remains under VA administrative jurisdiction.
Document 3: Amends the Grand Ronde Reservation Act to permit the Department of the Interior to accept real property located within the boundaries of the original 1857 reservation.
Interior must treat applications to take land into trust within the boundaries of the original 1857 reservation as on-reservation trust acquisitions.

topic.name: Education Policy and Support
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving educational access and support for diverse student populations.

Document 1: Strengthening Communities Through Education and Integration Act of 2008
integrated English literacy and civics education programs
Document 2: Student Nondiscrimination Act of 2010
prohibits public school students from being excluded from participating in educational programs
Document 3: Physician Assistant Higher Education Modernization Act of 2015
assist qualified individuals in receiving the education and training needed to become a physician assistant

topic.name: Housing and Employment Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving housing access and employment support for low-income individuals and families.

Document 1: Emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals
federal payments to states cover 100% of EUC.
Document 2: unlawful to discriminate in connection with the rental of a dwelling because the current or prospective tenant holds a housing voucher
HUD shall disclose on a departmental website information about each complaint received.
Document 3: expedite the application approval process for transferring ownership of Section 515 rural multifamily housing projects
housing and related facilities for elderly persons and families or other persons and families of low income.

topic.name: Legislative Acts and Agreements
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts and agreements aimed at addressing specific regional, economic, and social issues within the United States.

Document 1: Short Sea Shipping Act of 2009
exempt from the harbor maintenance tax commercial cargo
Document 2: Great Smoky Mountains National Park Agreement Act of 2014
transfer $4 million to counties within Great Smoky Mountains National Park
Document 3: Women's Business Ownership Act of 2009
establishes the Commission on Women's Business Ownership

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses the various benefits and support systems available to veterans, including eligibility for housing, burial, and transition assistance programs.

Document 1: includes as a homeless veteran, for purposes of eligibility for benefits through the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA)
fleeing domestic or dating violence, sexual assault, stalking, or other dangerous or life-threatening conditions
Document 2: Makes eligible for interment in a national cemetery any individual who: (1) the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) determines served in combat support of the Armed Forces
at the time of death was a U.S. citizen or lawfully admitted alien
Document 3: Wounded Warrior Transition Assistance Act of 2009
requires military human resources commands to return to active duty during their evaluation period reserve members who request such option

topic.name: Regulation of Minors and Substances
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at protecting minors and regulating controlled substances in commerce.

Document 1: Parents' Empowerment Act authorizes minors to bring civil actions for exposure to harmful entertainment material.
Affirmative defense for parents regarding minors' exposure to harmful material.
Document 2: Pre-Paid Mobile Device Identification Act requires verification of purchaser information to prevent misuse.
Imposes penalties for unauthorized sales of pre-paid mobile devices.
Document 3: Regulate Marijuana Like Alcohol Act seeks to remove marijuana from controlled substances schedules.
Amends federal laws to regulate marijuana similarly to alcohol.

topic.name: Federal Lands Management and Energy Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management of federal lands, energy resource development, and associated regulatory frameworks.

Document 1: encourages the use of U.S. workers and equipment manufactured in the U.S. in all construction related to mineral resource development
streamlining permitting process for onshore oil and gas drilling
Document 2: directs the Department of the Interior to finalize and publish feasibility studies for water storage and restoration projects
authorize construction of the Sites Reservoir in Colusa County if determined feasible
Document 3: terminates law enforcement functions on federal lands by federal employees
grants to states for maintaining law and order on federal land

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments for Small Businesses
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at modifying tax regulations for small businesses and certain taxpayers.

Document 1: expand the eligibility of certain small insurance companies for the alternative corporate income tax
increasing the premium limitation used to determine such eligibility to $2.025 million
Document 2: eliminate employment and self-employment taxes for small business owners
who employ 50 or fewer employees
Document 3: disallow the refundable portion of the child tax credit to taxpayers who use individual taxpayer identification numbers
to claim such credit on their tax returns
